title: After-Action Report — Agentic Audio: in-app "Game of the Week" broadcasts
scope: Stages 1 + 2 shipped, plus an auto-generate worker. In-app two-host broadcasts of (1) a single game ("🎧 Listen") and (2) a daily league roundup ("📻 Radio"), with a background worker that auto-narrates new game-days. Built as a separate `o27audio/` service wired into the live web app. Live API calls (Claude + OpenAI TTS) are first exercised on deploy; everything around them is verified offline.

rationale.item: The ask: an "MLB-TV-for-the-ears" companion — audio-only content narrating the leagues. Two flavors wanted: (1) a Game of the Week play-by-play call, and (2) a league "sports radio" roundup. It must run as a separate instance, not be baked into the sim, and must be usable entirely from the app UI on a phone, with no command line.

It's plumbing, not research. O27 games are seed-deterministic and already persist everything needed: `game_pbp.pbp_text` (~52k chars/game), plus `game_pa_log`, box stats, and `game_scoring_events`. The audio app never simulates.
Cost is modest and TTS-dominated. ~$0.50–$1 per game on the cheap baseline (OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini-tts`); Claude is the small part (~$0.15–$0.50). No GPUs, no always-on infra.

delegation_events.decision: Trigger and playback must live in the web UI, not a CLI
delegation_events.quote: "I don't use the command line for this I do everything inside the apps … I'm on a phone."

delegation_events.decision: Pre-rendered files (not live streaming), two-host booth, both content types, self-built pipeline (Claude + a TTS API) over an all-in-one podcast API
delegation_events.quote: Confirmed product choices (via Q&A): pre-rendered files (not live streaming), two-host booth, both content types (game + roundup), self-built pipeline (Claude + a TTS API) over an all-in-one podcast API.

actions.description: New self-contained o27audio/ package: sources.py (load_game, read-only), script.py (Claude structured outputs + stub_script), tts.py (OpenAI gpt-4o-mini-tts two voices + offline tone stub), render.py (stdlib wave stitch + MP3 via ffmpeg), manifest.py (sidecar index), pipeline.py (gather/produce split), blueprint.py (/audio generate/status/audio/player), cli.py (narrate-game with --dry-run/--stub-script/--stub-tts)
Web touch points (additive): register_blueprint(audio_bp); 🎧 Listen button on game page; Dockerfile installs ffmpeg; requirements add openai; .gitignore for generated output

Stage 2 roundup show (/audio/roundup, 📻 Radio in nav): day's slate, division leaders, season HR/RBI/K leaders, transactions wire; new manifest kind roundup keyed save_key:date; blueprint refactored to generic _launch/_status_payload/_serve_audio helpers + one shared player template

Auto-generate worker (o27audio/worker.py): daemon thread started by runserver, debounced game-day watcher, auto-generates roundup and (in full mode) day's most broadcast-worthy game via pick_game_of_the_day; cost guards via O27AUDIO_AUTOGEN env (roundup/full/off), one roundup per new date, failed render advances watermark

risk_posture: Built as a separate `o27audio/` service wired into the live web app ... nothing baked into the sim or core schema
Did not modify the sim, engine, or core schema. The audio service is a read-only consumer with its own sidecar manifest.
Background thread, not synchronous request ... An in-flight guard prevents double-taps.
Gather-in-request / produce-in-thread split ... so switching the active league mid-render can't corrupt the read.
WAV-first stitching ... avoids a hard ffmpeg/pydub dependency to build the clip
Multi-league safe. Clips are namespaced by the active save (save_key:game_id)

validation_claims.claim: Data layer: initdb + sim 5 into a throwaway DB; load_game pulls pbp (51,802 chars on the test game), 61 scoring events, 6 batting stars, pitching lines; --dry-run shows a 56k-char payload assembled correctly
validation_claims.check_method: Run initdb + sim and the CLI --dry-run against the throwaway DB

validation_claims.claim: Full pipeline offline: --stub-script --stub-tts produced a valid 68.3s, 24 kHz mono 16-bit WAV from 20 alternating turns, with a manifest row; second game rendered the same
validation_claims.check_method: Run the CLI with stub flags and inspect output WAV/manifest

validation_claims.claim: Web flow offline via Flask shim: none → generating → ok with duration/turns/cost, audio() resolved the file, player page rendered, repeat generate no-ops as ok
validation_claims.check_method: Drive blueprint view functions with a Flask shim or real Flask

validation_claims.claim: Safety/UX: missing-key and missing-SDK paths return friendly errors; bad game IDs 404; generated audio git-ignored; app.py compiles with the new import
validation_claims.check_method: Exercise error paths and compile app.py

validation_claims.claim: Worker validation: roundup dry-run assembles correct digest; full stub run produced a valid 24s roundup WAV; _tick driven directly — tick 1 observes/debounces, tick 2 generates, tick 3 no-ops — verified twice cleanly; refactored blueprint re-tested for both game and roundup
validation_claims.check_method: Drive worker._tick directly with stubbed pipeline

non_actions.reason: no sandbox network — voice quality and prompt tuning are the first post-deploy task

non_actions.item: Modifying the sim, engine, or core schema
non_actions.reason: The audio service is a read-only consumer with its own sidecar manifest.
validation was manual (offline harness). A small test that exercises `pipeline.produce(..., stub=True)` would be a cheap follow-up.

non_actions.item: Admin UI/kill-switch for the worker beyond the env var
non_actions.reason: the worker has no admin UI/kill-switch beyond the env var

residual_risks: The two live API calls are unproven from here ... first real contact is on deploy. Voice quality, persona steering, and the script's handling of O27-specific mechanics (Second Chance, Walk-Back, Super-Innings) need a real listen and likely prompt tuning.
Requires an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Fly secret. Only OpenAI was set; the scripting step errors without the Claude key (surfaced on the page, not a crash).
Latency is real. First render of a game is ~a minute; subsequent plays are instant (cached on volume).
Cost-estimate is approximate; reconcile against the dashboard before trusting it for a backfill.
max_pbp_chars cap (60k): unusually long games are truncated before the box-score tail; a very long game loses its final innings of detail unless the cap is raised.
open_hooks: Deploy + listen: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, merge #212, tune the two voices and booth personas from the first sample
A/B the TTS provider: provider sits behind one interface; compare OpenAI vs ElevenLabs by ear vs cost
pipeline.produce*(..., stub=True) test is the obvious cheap follow-up
Manifest stores char_count and est_cost_usd per clip to reconcile against provider dashboards before any bulk run
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Agentic Audio: in-app "Game of the Week" broadcasts
Cost (recorded per clip in the manifest)
Validation (what was actually verified, and how)
Stage 2 + worker (built after the initial report)
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/agentic-audio-sports-app-2o2jM (PR #212). | Update (same session): the "what I did NOT do" items below — the roundup show and the auto-generate worker — were subsequently built. | Tools table: Anthropic Claude (anthropic SDK, Opus 4.8, structured outputs, prompt caching); OpenAI gpt-4o-mini-tts (two voice IDs, per-host tone via instructions); stdlib wave + ffmpeg; SQLite via o27v2.db read-only; Fly volume /data/audio + sidecar manifest.db. | Cost detail: ~30k-char script ≈ 12–18 spoken min; script $0.15–$0.50 (Sonnet→Opus; Batch API halves it); TTS ~$0.45–$0.50 OpenAI vs ElevenLabs ~$1.50 (Flash) to ~$9 (premium); 100-game backfill ≈ $50–$100; roundup mode ~$0.20–$0.60 per in-game day, full adds ~$0.50–$1. | Persistence: when O27V2_SAVES_DIR is set (Fly), clips write to /data/audio; otherwise o27audio/out/. | Original 'What I did NOT do' included the roundup show and auto-generate worker, later built (recorded as actions).
parser_notes: Document updated within the same session: two original non-actions (roundup show, auto-generate worker) were subsequently built and reported in a 'Stage 2 + worker' section; remaining non-actions taken from the updated 'Still not done' list plus unrevised items.
